# 44Mesh Documentation

> BGP routing + ZeroTier overlay mesh giving any device a publicly reachable IPv4 address. No NAT, no cloud.

## Docs

- [What is 44Mesh](docs/overview/what-is-44mesh.md): Problem, solution, use cases, AMPRNet 44/8
- [Architecture](docs/overview/architecture.md): System layers, traffic flows, containers
- [Design Principles](docs/overview/design-principles.md): Sovereignty, public reachability, automation
- [Prerequisites](docs/getting-started/prerequisites.md): What you need to deploy
- [Quickstart](docs/getting-started/quickstart.md): End-to-end deployment walkthrough
- [Addressing](docs/network/addressing.md): IPv4 allocation and address roles
- [BGP Routing](docs/network/bgp.md): BIRD2, eBGP sessions, route filters
- [ZeroTier Mesh](docs/network/zerotier-mesh.md): Overlay mesh, AlterMundi fork, ingress routing
- [Routing & Policy](docs/network/routing.md): Source-based policy routing
- [Border Router](docs/deployment/border-router.md): Border router deployment
- [Mesh Nodes](docs/deployment/mesh-nodes.md): Mesh node deployment
- [Observation API](docs/ai-agents/observation-api.md): REST API for sensor data
- [Data Model](docs/ai-agents/data-model.md): Observation/capability/alert schemas
- [Glossary](docs/references/glossary.md): Key term definitions
